channel.title: The New Beverly Cinema
channel.description: Schedule and program for The New Beverly Cinema (Quentin Tarantino's revival theater in Los Angeles).

item.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
item.description:
In the second film in the blockbuster franchise, our dashing daredevil is tasked with locating a sacred stone and saving kidnapped children. With a pre-teen sidekick (Ke Huy Quan) and a nightclub singer (Kate Capshaw) at his side, Indy embarks on another mission, that while darker and more violent, maintains the non-stop excitement, jaw-dropping stunt work, and smile-inducing exuberance of the original. “Steven Spielberg’s Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom is one of the greatest Bruised Forearm Movies ever made. You know what a Bruised Forearm Movie is. That’s the kind of movie where your date is always grabbing your forearm in a viselike grip, as unbearable excitement unfolds on the screen. After the movie is over, you’ve had a great time but your arm is black-and-blue for a week.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Steven Spielberg | Writer: Screenplay by Willard Huyck & Gloria Katz, story by George Lucas | Starring: Harrison Ford, Kate Capshaw, Ke Huy Quan, Amrish Puri | Year: 1984 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 118 minuti

Orari: Sun May 03 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Unstoppable / The Taking of Pelham 123
item.description:
1 million tons of steel. 100,000 lives at stake. 100 minutes to impact. All hope rides on the courage of two men when a half-mile long freight train laden with toxic chemicals races out of control. Denzel Washington & Chris Pine star as a veteran engineer and a young conductor, our last hope in stopping an Unstoppable locomotive in Tony Scott’s relentlessly thrilling action nail-biter. Walter Garber (Denzel Washington) is a New York City subway dispatcher whose ordinary day is thrown into chaos by an audacious crime: the hijacking of a subway train. Ryder (John Travolta), the criminal mastermind behind the hijacking and leader of a highly-armed gang of four, threatens to execute the train’s passengers unless a large ransom is paid within one hour. As the tension mounts beneath his feet, Garber employs his vast knowledge of the subway system in a battle to outwit Ryder and save the hostages. But there’s one riddle Garber can’t solve: even if the thieves get the money, how can they possibly escape? (Sony)
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Mark Bomback | Starring: Denzel Washington, Chris Pine, Rosario Dawson, Ethan Suplee, Kevin Dunn, Kevin Corrigan | Year: 2010 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Screenplay by Brian Helgeland based on the novel by John Godey | Starring: Denzel Washington, John Travolta, John Turturro, Luis Guzman, Michael Rispoli, James Gandolfini | Year: 2009 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 106 minuti

Orari: Sat May 02 - 6:00 pm / 8:10 pm

item.title: Unstoppable / The Taking of Pelham 123
item.description:
1 million tons of steel. 100,000 lives at stake. 100 minutes to impact. All hope rides on the courage of two men when a half-mile long freight train laden with toxic chemicals races out of control. Denzel Washington & Chris Pine star as a veteran engineer and a young conductor, our last hope in stopping an Unstoppable locomotive in Tony Scott’s relentlessly thrilling action nail-biter. Walter Garber (Denzel Washington) is a New York City subway dispatcher whose ordinary day is thrown into chaos by an audacious crime: the hijacking of a subway train. Ryder (John Travolta), the criminal mastermind behind the hijacking and leader of a highly-armed gang of four, threatens to execute the train’s passengers unless a large ransom is paid within one hour. As the tension mounts beneath his feet, Garber employs his vast knowledge of the subway system in a battle to outwit Ryder and save the hostages. But there’s one riddle Garber can’t solve: even if the thieves get the money, how can they possibly escape? (Sony)
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Mark Bomback | Starring: Denzel Washington, Chris Pine, Rosario Dawson, Ethan Suplee, Kevin Dunn, Kevin Corrigan | Year: 2010 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Screenplay by Brian Helgeland based on the novel by John Godey | Starring: Denzel Washington, John Travolta, John Turturro, Luis Guzman, Michael Rispoli, James Gandolfini | Year: 2009 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 106 minuti

Orari: Fri May 01 - 6:00 pm / 8:10 pm

item.title: Make Me a Star / Don’t Gamble with Strangers
item.description:
Stars in their eyes – laughter in the aisles! Famed funnyman Stuart Erwin portrays smalltown hick and Hollywood hopeful Merton Gill in the comedic tale that was first a novel, a beloved (and periodically reprised) stage hit, and silent film. Erwin adds a wistful note to Merton’s Tinseltown dream in 1932’s Make Me a Star , including star cameos including then-current luminaries Tallulah Bankhead, Maurice Chevalier, Claudette Colbert, and Gary Cooper. (Warner Bros) When Mike Sarno (Kane Richmond) out-cheats Fay Benton (Bernadene Hayes) in a crooked poker game, the two gamblers bury the hatchet and agree to become partners in crime. Posing as brother and sister, the pair finally hit the big time when Mike runs into a gambler (Phil Van Zandt) who owes him a debt and takes over his casino to square it. As the high-rollers’ dough starts pouring in, Mike eventually tires of Fay and dumps her to get his hands on a gorgeous heiress (Gloria Warren) and her cash, a double-cross that will lead to murder and a shocking surprise when the cops reveal the killer’s name. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Sam Mintz & Walter Deleon and Arthur Kober based on the play “Merton of the Movies” by George S. Kaufman and Marc Connelly and the novel by Harry Leon Wilson | Starring: Joan Blondell, Stuart Erwin, Zasu Pitts, Ben Turpin | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Kane Richmond, Bernadene Hayes, Peter Cookson, Gloria Warren | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 67 minuti

Orari: Thu April 30 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm

item.title: You’re Not So Tough / Hit the Road
item.description:
HOLD EVERYTHING… the gang’s here again! Fists fly and jaws crack! As the “Kids” and the “Guys” join forces to round up a racket ring in the rowdiest, roughest, fastest, funniest package of dynamite entertainment. Out of the slums of NYC, our scrappy crew find themselves riding the rails and, ultimately land at a California ranch, where their kindly host’s backstory could create complications… and pull at their heart strings. The mob meets its match and the toughies get the tender treatment in their most dramatic adventure! The Dead End Kids and Little Tough Guys want out of their juvenile reformatory, but no one wants them because they’re the orphans of gangland criminals. Their superintendent has a plan: release the kids to the newly reformed, fresh-out-of-prison former leader of their parents’ gang. But has their new sponsor truly gone good or is it all an act? And what do his former mob associates have in store for him?
Cast: Director: Joe May | Writer: Screenplay by Arthur T. Horman based on a story by Maxwell Aley | Starring: Nan Grey, Billy Halop, Huntz Hall, Gabriel Dell, Bernard Punsly, Bobby Jordon, Henry Armetta, Rosina Galli | Year: 1940 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe May | Writer: Screenplay by Robert Lee Johnson, Brenda Weisberg, Original story by Robert Lee Johnson | Starring: Gladys George, Barton MacLane, Billy Halop, Huntz Hall, Gabriel Dell, Bernard Punsly, Evelyn Ankers, Charles Lang, Shemp Howard, Bobs Watson | Year: 1941 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 61 minuti

Orari: Mon May 04 - 7:30 pm / 9:05 pm

item.title: Savage Streets
item.description:
When Brenda (Linda Blair, grown up and brutally foul-mouthed), her sister ( Return of the Living Dead ’s Linnea Quigley), and their crew the Satins aren’t busy having fun or battling the authority of their iron-fisted high school principal, they’re cruising the Savage Streets of Hollywood, pulling pranks and drinking Schnapps. But when vicious, leather-clad punks push their fun too far, it will ignite a violently vengeful gang war of the sexes.
Cast: Director: Danny Steinmann | Writer: Screenplay by Norman Yonemoto and Danny Steinmann | Starring: Linda Blair, John Vernon, Robert Dryer, Johnny Venocur, Sal Landi, Linnea Quigley | Year: 1984 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 93 minuti

Orari: Sat May 02 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
item.description:
In the second film in the blockbuster franchise, our dashing daredevil is tasked with locating a sacred stone and saving kidnapped children. With a pre-teen sidekick (Ke Huy Quan) and a nightclub singer (Kate Capshaw) at his side, Indy embarks on another mission, that while darker and more violent, maintains the non-stop excitement, jaw-dropping stunt work, and smile-inducing exuberance of the original. “Steven Spielberg’s Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom is one of the greatest Bruised Forearm Movies ever made. You know what a Bruised Forearm Movie is. That’s the kind of movie where your date is always grabbing your forearm in a viselike grip, as unbearable excitement unfolds on the screen. After the movie is over, you’ve had a great time but your arm is black-and-blue for a week.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Steven Spielberg | Writer: Screenplay by Willard Huyck & Gloria Katz, story by George Lucas | Starring: Harrison Ford, Kate Capshaw, Ke Huy Quan, Amrish Puri | Year: 1984 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 118 minuti

Orari: Sat May 02 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Unstoppable / The Taking of Pelham 123
item.description:
1 million tons of steel. 100,000 lives at stake. 100 minutes to impact. All hope rides on the courage of two men when a half-mile long freight train laden with toxic chemicals races out of control. Denzel Washington & Chris Pine star as a veteran engineer and a young conductor, our last hope in stopping an Unstoppable locomotive in Tony Scott’s relentlessly thrilling action nail-biter. Walter Garber (Denzel Washington) is a New York City subway dispatcher whose ordinary day is thrown into chaos by an audacious crime: the hijacking of a subway train. Ryder (John Travolta), the criminal mastermind behind the hijacking and leader of a highly-armed gang of four, threatens to execute the train’s passengers unless a large ransom is paid within one hour. As the tension mounts beneath his feet, Garber employs his vast knowledge of the subway system in a battle to outwit Ryder and save the hostages. But there’s one riddle Garber can’t solve: even if the thieves get the money, how can they possibly escape? (Sony)
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Mark Bomback | Starring: Denzel Washington, Chris Pine, Rosario Dawson, Ethan Suplee, Kevin Dunn, Kevin Corrigan | Year: 2010 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Screenplay by Brian Helgeland based on the novel by John Godey | Starring: Denzel Washington, John Travolta, John Turturro, Luis Guzman, Michael Rispoli, James Gandolfini | Year: 2009 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 106 minuti

Orari: Sun May 03 - 6:30 pm / 8:40 pm

item.title: Make Me a Star / Don’t Gamble with Strangers
item.description:
Stars in their eyes – laughter in the aisles! Famed funnyman Stuart Erwin portrays smalltown hick and Hollywood hopeful Merton Gill in the comedic tale that was first a novel, a beloved (and periodically reprised) stage hit, and silent film. Erwin adds a wistful note to Merton’s Tinseltown dream in 1932’s Make Me a Star , including star cameos including then-current luminaries Tallulah Bankhead, Maurice Chevalier, Claudette Colbert, and Gary Cooper. (Warner Bros) When Mike Sarno (Kane Richmond) out-cheats Fay Benton (Bernadene Hayes) in a crooked poker game, the two gamblers bury the hatchet and agree to become partners in crime. Posing as brother and sister, the pair finally hit the big time when Mike runs into a gambler (Phil Van Zandt) who owes him a debt and takes over his casino to square it. As the high-rollers’ dough starts pouring in, Mike eventually tires of Fay and dumps her to get his hands on a gorgeous heiress (Gloria Warren) and her cash, a double-cross that will lead to murder and a shocking surprise when the cops reveal the killer’s name. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Sam Mintz & Walter Deleon and Arthur Kober based on the play “Merton of the Movies” by George S. Kaufman and Marc Connelly and the novel by Harry Leon Wilson | Starring: Joan Blondell, Stuart Erwin, Zasu Pitts, Ben Turpin | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Kane Richmond, Bernadene Hayes, Peter Cookson, Gloria Warren | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 67 minuti

Orari: Wed April 29 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm

item.title: Django Unchained
item.description:
Quentin Tarantino’s hit western stars Jamie Foxx as Django Unchained , a slave freed by bounty hunter Dr. King Schultz (Christoph Waltz) who needs his help tracking down a trio of wanted outlaws. In exchange, Schultz agrees to help Django free his wife from evil plantation owner Calvin Candie (Leonard DiCaprio). To a bounty hunter it’s just business but to Django it’s personal! “In Django , Tarantino is a man unchained , creating his most articulate, intriguing, provoking, appalling, hilarious, exhilarating, scathing and downright entertaining film yet.” – Betsy Sharkey, Los Angeles Times
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Jamie Foxx, Christoph Waltz, Leonardo DiCaprio, Kerry Washington, Samuel L. Jackson, Walton Goggins, Dennis Christopher, James Remar | Year: 2012 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 165 minuti

Orari: Fri May 01 - 11:59 pm
